title: Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
description:
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100.

The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.

The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. Step Name:Final result of what you are going to built
Detailed Instructions:

Insight: 

Tips and Tricks for Effective Power BI Dashboard

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Importing Data
Detailed Instructions:

Data Set Link:  https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/heemalichaudhari/adidas-sales-dataset

1.    First you need to have the data save on your device.

2.    Open Power BI Desktop.
3.    Click on Get data ribbon
4.    Select Excel Workbook and then click connect
5.    Choose “Adidas US Sales Datasets” and  click open
6.    Select the table, a preview of data will be displayed, click on Load. 
Follow the Gif below for step by step.

Insight: 

Import Excel workbooks into Power BI

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Data Transformation
Detailed Instructions:
In this step you will clean and transform the data:
1.  Select Transform Data from Home tab
2.  Click on the down arrow of Column1 and unselect the “null” then click OK, to                 remove the first few null rows which only have the title ” Adidas Sales Database” 
3.  Click on Use First Row as Headers, to make the first row as a header of the columns
4.  Click on File and select Close and Apply.
Follow the Gif below for step by step.

Insight: 

Transforming Data

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Creating Calendar Table
Detailed Instructions:

1.    Go to Modeling Tab click on new table.
2.    Type the following DAX formula on DAX formula bar, click on the check mark and save.

Calendar = ADDCOLUMNS (
CALENDAR (Date(Year(Min('Data Sales Adidas'[Invoice Date])),
Month(Min('Data Sales Adidas'[Invoice Date])),
DAY(Min('Data Sales Adidas'[Invoice Date]))),
Date(Year(Max('Data Sales Adidas'[Invoice Date])),
Month(Max('Data Sales Adidas'[Invoice Date])),
DAY(Max('Data Sales Adidas'[Invoice Date])))),
"Month number", FORMAT ([Date], "MM"),
"Month Year", FORMAT ([Date], "MMMYYYY"),
"Month", FORMAT ([Date], "mmmm"),
"Week Number", "Week-"& WEEKNUM([Date]),
"Week", WEEKNUM ([Date]),
"Day Of Month", Month([DATE]) &"-"& FORMAT ( [Date], "dd" ),
"Year Quarter", FORMAT ([Date], "YYYY") & "/Q" & FORMAT ([Date], "Q"),
"Year", FORMAT ([Date], "YYYY")
)
Follow the Gif below for step by step.

Insight: 

How to Create Calendar Table

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Creating Refresh Date Table
Detailed Instructions:

1.    On Power BI Desktop ribbon, Click on Transform data
2.    Click on New Source button, and select Blank query from the list and click on Connect
3.    Under properties in the Name box type in 'Refresh Date'
4.    On the DAX formula bar type ' =DateTime.LocalNow()'
5.    Click on Convert to Table button
6.    After the refresh table pops up, rename the column by right clicking and choosing           rename from the selection
7.    Click on Close & apply button and save
Follow the Gif below for step by step.

Insight: 

How to Create Refreshed Date Table

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Data Model
Detailed Instructions:

1.   Go to the data modeling page.                       
2.   Drag and connect the ‘Invoice Date’ column of the ‘Data Sales Adidas’ Table to the ‘Calendar’ Table to create relationship between the tables.
Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Insight: 

How to Create Relationship in Power BI

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Creating All Measures Table with Measures
Detailed Instructions:

1.  In Home Tab, select Enter data and Create New table
2.  Rename the table to ‘All Measures’ Table
3.  Click Load
4.  Right Click on 'All Measures' and select New Measure
5.  Copy and paste the DAX formula for TotalSales Measure on the Dax Bar 
6.  Click on the Right mark
7.  Repeat step 4-6 until you create all measures using the below Dax formula.
Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Measures:
Some of the measures need formatting, use the green highlighted text to format that measure (it is not part of the DAX)

1.   TotalSales = SUM('Data Sales Adidas'[Total Sales])
            [format-Whole number with 0 places and use comma(,)] 
 
2.   Total Sales LM = CALCULATE(SUM('Data Sales Adidas'[Total Sales]),                                  DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,MONTH))
3.   Sales MoM% = DIVIDE(([TotalSales]-Total Sales LM),Total Sales LM)
            [format-percentage with 1 places] 
 
4.   Arrow Sales MoM% = SWITCH(TRUE(), [Sales MoM%] <0, UNICHAR(9660),                    UNICHAR(9650))
 
5.   Sales Per Month = DIVIDE([TotalSales], DISTINCTCOUNT('Calendar'[Month                      Year]),0)+0
6.   Profit = SUM('Data Sales Adidas'[Operating Profit])
             [format-Whole number with 0 places and use comma(,)] 
 
7.   OPM = DIVIDE([Profit],[TotalSales],0)
             [format-percentage with 2 places]

8.   OPM LM = CALCULATE([OPM],DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,MONTH))
             [format-percentage with 2 places]

9.   OPM MOM% = DIVIDE(([OPM]- OPM LM),OPM LM)
             [format-percentage with 2 places]

10.  Arrow OPM MOM% = SWITCH(TRUE(),[OPM MOM%]<0,
          UNICHAR(9660),UNICHAR(9650))
Insight: 

How to Create Measure Table

With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. Step Name:Adding Canvas Background
Detailed Instructions:

1.     Click on anywhere on canvas, go to Format page in visualizations, and click on Canvas background.
2.     In the Image click Browse and select the image and click open.
3.     Set Image Fit = Fit, Transparency = 82%
Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Insight: 

How to Add Custom Background to Power BI Canvas

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Adding Project title, Company Logo and Adidas logo
Detailed Instructions:

1.   In the report view, Select Insert ribbon>Text box 
     a.   Type the Project Name ‘Adidas US Sales 2020 – 2021’ 
     b.   Highlight the Text and adjust Size = 28, Font = Segoe (Bold), Center 
     c.    In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height= 96, Width = 562,              Position: Horizontal = 283, Vertical = 0, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency =0% 
2.   In the Report View, Select Insert ribbon > Image > and select the Company logo. 
     a.    In the Formatting Tab-->General--> Properties-->Size: Height = 96, Width = 210, Position: Horizontal = 1, Vertical = 0, Background: Off. 
3.   Repeat the same step 2 for Adidas Logo 
      a.  In the Formatting Tab-->General--> Properties-->Size: Height = 96, Width =187, Position: Horizontal = 1093, Vertical = 0, Background: Off. 
  Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Insight: 

How to create Custom Images and Title

Step Name:Adding Date Slicer
Detailed Instructions:

1.  In the Report View, open the visualization pane, then Select Slicer from visualization
2.  In the fields, open ‘Calendar’ Table, drag the Date.
    a. In the Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Values: Font = Segoe UI Bold, Size = 9, Slider: On
    b. In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height = 96, Width = 247,            Position: Horizontal = 854, Vertical = 0, Title: Date, Font = Segoe UI Bold, Size =15,          Horizontal alignment= Center.
  Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Insight: 

How to Create Date Slicer

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. Step Name:Adding Main KPI Cards
Detailed Instructions:

1.    Click on an empty space on the canvas, and from the visualizations pane select Visual Card.
2.    Drag the Total Sales to the fields value box.
3.    In the Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold, size = 23, Category label: Off
4.    In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height = 74, Width = 210, Position: Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 99, Title: Total Sales, Font = Segoe UI Bold, Size = 15, Color = White, Background Color= Black, Center, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency = 100%
5.    For the 2nd Main KPI copy and paste the first one and Drag Profit to the fields value box
6.    In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height = 74, Width = 210, Position: Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 218, Title: Profit, Font = Segoe UI Bold, Size = 15, Color = White, Background Color= Black, Center, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency = 100%.

Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Insight: 

How to create KPI Cards

Step Name:Adding Sub KPI Cards
Detailed Instructions:

1.   Click on canvas, click card visualizations, drag Sales per Month to fields value box, 
     a.  In the format-> Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold, size =18, color #118DFF, Category label= Off
     b.  In the format->General-->Properties-->Size: Height =46, Width = 98, Position:               Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 172, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency= 100%.
2.  For the 2nd Sub KPI copy and paste the first Sub KPI and Drag Sales MoM% to the fields value box
    a.   In the format-> Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold size =18, color #D9B300, Category label= Off
    b.   In the format->General-->Properties-->Size: Height =46, Width = 98, Position:                Horizontal = 112, Vertical = 172, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency = 100%.
3.  For the 3rd Sub KPI copy and paste the 2nd sub KPI then Drag OPM to the fields value box
    a.   In the format-> Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold size =18, color #AD5129, Category label= Off
    b.  In the format->General-->Properties-->Size: Height =46, Width = 98, Position:               Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 295, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency  = 100%. 
4.   For the 4th Sub KPI copy and paste the 2nd sub KPI then Drag OPM MoM% to the        fields value box
     a.   In the format-> Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold size =18, color #12239E, Category label= Off
     b.  In the format->General-->Properties-->Size: Height =46, Width = 105, Position:              Horizontal = 105, Vertical = 295, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency = 100%.

Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Step Name:Adding Indicators
Detailed Instructions:

1.  Click on canvas, click Card visualizations, drag Arrow Sales MoM% to fields value box
    a.  In the format-> Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold, size =28,
         color -> click fx (conditional formatting) set format style= rules, field based on = Sales MoM %,
            If value >=0 and <=max then #25AB2D
            If value >=min and <0 then #DB1B29,
        Category label= Off
    b. In the format->General-->Properties-->Size: Height =36, Width = 68, Position:                Horizontal = 144, Vertical = 146, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency = 100%.
2.  For the 2nd indicator copy paste the first one and drag Arrow OPM MoM% to fields value box, 
    a. In the format-> Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Callout value: Font = Segoe UI Bold, size =28,
        color -> click fx(conditional formatting) set format style= rules, field based on = OPM MoM %,
            If value >=0 and <=max then #25AB2D
            If value >=min and <0 then #DB1B29,
        Category label= Off
    b.  In the format->General-->Properties-->Size: Height =36, Width = 68, Position:               Horizontal = 144, Vertical = 267, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency = 100%.
Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI

With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Creating Mth/Qtr/Yr Chart
Detailed Instructions:

Click on an empty space on the canvas, and from the visualizations pane select Line and Clustered Column Chart.
Drag Year, Year Quarter, and Month Year column from Calendar table to the X- axis. Followed by Sales Per Mth Measure on Column y-axis, then drag the following measures into Line y-axis  (Sales MoM%, OPM and OPM MOM %) 
Drag all these measures into the Tooltips field: (Sales MoM%, OPM and OPM MOM%)
In the Formatting Tab-->x-axis: On, Font= Segoe UI Bold, size= 9, Color = Black. Title: Off.          Y-axis: On, Font = Segoe UI Bold, size= 9, Color= Black, Display units=Millions. Title: Off.      Secondary y-axis: On, Align zeros: on, Font= Segoe UI Bold, size= 9, Color = Black. Title: Off.      Legend: On, Position = Top left, Text: Font= Segoe UI Bold, size= 10, Color = Black. Title: On.    Gridlines: Horizontal: On style = Solid, Color= #F3F2F1, width = 1.                                      Columns: Colors= #118DFF.                                                                                                                Line: Shape= Solid, Colors:  Sales MoM% = #D9B300, OPM% = #AD5129, OPM MoM% = #12239E, Shaded area: On.                                                                                                                   Data labels: On, series=Sales Per Mth, show data labels=on, position =outside end, values: font=segoe UI(9), color= Black, background= Background: On, Color = White, Transparency=30%.  Data labels for all other lines turned off.
In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height = 317, Width = 634, Position: Horizontal = 211, Vertical = 99, Title: Mth/Qtr/Yr, Font = Segoe UI Bold, Size = 14, Color = White, Background: Black, Center, Effect: Background: On, Color = White, Transparency=50%. 
       Tooltips: On, Text Font = Segoe UI Bold, Size = 10, Label Color = Black, Value Color = Black and             Background: Color=White

Insight: 

How to Create Combo Chart

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.

Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
Adidas AG is one of the largest sportswear manufacturers in the world, with a strong presence in Europe and second only to Nike globally. With a brand value of 16.5 billion U.S. dollars, it is the fourth-largest apparel brand in the world, and it is considered as one of the most valuable athletic apparel, accessories, and footwear companies. In 2021, Adidas surpassed Nike in terms of customer satisfaction in America consumers, achieving a score of 79 out of 100. The purpose of this project is to analyze Adidas USA sales between 2020 and 2021. The report will utilize key performance indicators such as total sales, sales per month, sales month-over-month percentage, total operating profit, operating profit margin, and operating profit margin month-over-month percentage to analyze the sales and profit of the company. This analysis will help the company understand the performance of their sales and take necessary actions to improve it.
Step Name:Creating Location Map & Legend
Detailed Instructions:

1.  Copy and paste Sales Method chart, change visual type in to Shape Map, on Location add State Column from ‘Data Sales Adidas’ Table, delete Sales Method from the Legend. 
2.  In the Formatting Tab-->Visual-->Fill colors: Gradient=On, Minimum=#FFFFFF, Center= #70BBFF, Maximum=#118DFF, Minimum=250,000, Center= 1,500,000, Maximum=2,500,000.
3.  Take screenshot of the Gradient and save it in your device so that you can use it later as a Legend for the Map.
4.  In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height=317, Width=434, Position, Horizontal= 845, Vertical=99. Title=On, Text=Location.
5.  To create the Map Legend. In the report view, Select Insert ribbon>Text box >Type         ‘Low and High’, Highlight the Text and adjust Size = 10, Font = Segoe (Bold), color=  Black.
6.  In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height=29, Width=212, Position, Horizontal= 1054, Vertical=375. Title=Off, Effects: Background: Color= #E6E6E6.
7.  In the report view, Select Insert ribbon>Image > and select the Gradient Image you created earlier at no. 3.
8.  In the Formatting Tab-->General-->Properties-->Size: Height = 29, Width = 115, Position: Horizontal = 1093, Vertical = 375, Background: Off.
9.  Select the Gradient image the text and the Map by holding shift key then go to Format tab select Group.
Follow the following Gif for step by step instruction.

portfolioContent.title: Adidas US Sales Dashboard in Power BI
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a Power BI dashboard to analyze Adidas USA sales and profitability trends from 2020 to 2021.
portfolioContent.summary: This project involved creating a comprehensive Power BI dashboard to analyze Adidas USA sales performance between 2020 and 2021. Key performance indicators such as total sales, monthly sales, operating profit, and profit margins were visualized to provide actionable business insights. The dashboard enables stakeholders to monitor sales trends and profitability to support data-driven decisions.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Adidas needed to understand its sales and profit performance in the US market to identify growth opportunities and areas for improvement. With increasing competition, especially from Nike, analyzing sales data was critical to maintain market position and enhance profitability. The challenge was to consolidate and visualize complex sales metrics effectively for strategic decision-making.
objectives: Analyze Adidas USA sales and profit trends from 2020 to 2021
Visualize key performance indicators including sales, operating profit, and margins
Enable data-driven insights to support business growth and operational improvements
workflow: Import sales data into Power BI from Kaggle dataset
Clean and transform data using Power Query Editor
Create calendar and refresh date tables for time intelligence
Build data model by establishing relationships between tables
Develop DAX measures and design the final dashboard for visualization
keyInsights: Monthly sales trends reveal seasonal fluctuations impacting revenue
Operating profit margins showed month-over-month variations indicating cost management opportunities
Adidas surpassed Nike in US customer satisfaction in 2021, reflecting strong brand positioning
Sales and profit data highlight key periods for targeted marketing and inventory planning
businessImpact: Improved visibility into sales and profitability trends for strategic planning
Enabled timely identification of performance gaps and growth opportunities
Supported data-driven decision-making to enhance Adidas' competitive position in the US market
